subject: West Coast Power Project - Go Decision
body:
Ken, Andy,

After reviewing the risk models with Greg Whalley, I've decided we should move forward with the West Coast Power project. Target capacity is 800 MW. We'll need Andy to close the Raptors financing by end of Q1.

Assigning project lead to Louise Kitchen. She has full authority to negotiate contracts.

Let's not discuss this over email going forward.

- Jeff

subject: RE: West Coast Power Project - Go Decision
body:
Jeff,

Agreed. The Board approved up to $200M for this. Louise is a solid choice. Make sure Greg is looped in on risk limits - we can't have another California situation.

Andy, please get the Raptors structure finalized. I want to see the term sheet before Christmas.

Ken

subject: West Coast Power - initial scope and team
body:
Jeff, Greg,

Thanks for the vote of confidence. Here's the initial plan:

Project: West Coast Power (internal code: WCP-1)
Target: 800 MW by Q3 2002
Budget: $200M (confirmed with Andy)
Team: pulling in Tim Belden for trading strategy, John Lavorato for ops

First milestone: counterparty term sheets by Dec 20.

Will keep Greg posted on risk exposure daily.

- Louise

subject: RE: West Coast Power - initial scope and team
body:
Louise,

Glad to be on this. Quick flag: I've been talking to regulators in Sacramento about load scheduling constraints. The 800 MW target may be aggressive given transmission limits out of Oregon. We might need to revise down to 600 MW or look at Nevada routing.

Let me set up a call with Greg before we commit the term sheets.

- Tim

subject: WCP-1 Risk Review
body:
Louise, Tim,

Reviewed Tim's transmission concern. Risk committee met this morning. Decision: we hold at 800 MW but add a Nevada corridor option as fallback. If transmission is constrained by Feb 1, we automatically drop to 620 MW.

This is now locked in as the project risk envelope. Greg

subject: Raptors financing - term sheet attached
body:
Jeff, Ken,

Term sheet for Raptors III is ready. Structure: $180M SPE with Enron stock as collateral. Closes Dec 28 if we sign by Dec 15.

One issue: LJM2 will need to co-invest $30M. I can manage that separately.

Andy

subject: RE: Raptors financing - term sheet attached
body:
Andy,

Approved. Get the LJM2 piece done on your end. Don't need to see the details.

Jeff

subject: WCP-1 status update
body:
Team,

Update as of Dec 10:
- Three counterparties engaged: PG&E, SoCal Edison, Nevada Power
- PG&E is slow due to bankruptcy proceedings - may drop them
- SoCal Edison and Nevada Power both interested at $42/MWh
- Tim: transmission issue tabled pending FERC ruling

Target: close at least 2 contracts by Dec 20.

Louise

subject: RE: WCP-1 status update
body:
Louise,

Drop PG&E. They can't sign anything while in Chapter 11. Nevada Power is the better bet - they have a signed LOI from last year with EES (Enron Energy Services) that gives us favorable terms.

Also heads up: John Arnold is asking questions about our load scheduling strategy out of Portland. You may want to loop him in or shut that down.

- Lavo

subject: Decision: drop PG&E, focus Nevada Power + SoCal
body:
Agreed on PG&E - dropping them from scope. Confirmed targets are Nevada Power and SoCal Edison.

On John Arnold: he's fine, he's just nosy. Not looping him in.

- Louise

subject: Decision: drop PG&E, focus Nevada Power + SoCal
body:
Agreed on PG&E - dropping them from scope. Confirmed targets are Nevada Power and SoCal Edison.

On John Arnold: he's fine, he's just nosy. Not looping him in.

- Louise

subject: WCP-1 - Board wants project paused
body:
Jeff, Louise,

The Board had an emergency session this morning. Given Enron's current liquidity situation, they're asking us to pause all new capital commitments over $100M pending a review in January.

WCP-1 is on hold. Do not sign any contracts. I'm sorry - this was not my call.

Ken

subject: RE: WCP-1 - Board wants project paused
body:
Ken,

This reverses the Dec 5 go-decision. I understand the situation but Louise and the team have done real work here. We owe them a clear explanation.

Also - what happens to the Raptors III close on Dec 28? Andy has already committed LJM2 funds.

Jeff

subject: Raptors III - unwinding
body:
Jeff,

Raptors III will need to be unwound. LJM2 can absorb the $30M but the SPE unwind is messier. Working with legal on it. 

This stays between us for now.

Andy

subject: WCP-1 paused - team update
body:
Team,

Board has paused WCP-1 effective immediately. No contracts to be signed. Counterparties to be notified by EOD today.

This is a hold, not a cancel. We may revisit in January.

Your work has been excellent. Thank you.

Louise

subject: A message to all employees
body:
Team,

I want to be transparent: Enron is facing serious financial difficulties. We are working with our advisors to explore all options. Employee 401k plans are not at risk in the short term.

I remain committed to this company.

Kenneth Lay
Chairman and CEO

subject: A message to all employees
body:
Team,

I want to be transparent: Enron is facing serious financial difficulties. We are working with our advisors to explore all options. Employee 401k plans are not at risk in the short term.

I remain committed to this company.

Kenneth Lay
Chairman and CEO

subject: WCP-1 officially cancelled
body:
Louise,

Heard from Ken's office: WCP-1 is cancelled, not just paused. Enron filed Chapter 11 this morning. The project is dead.

Greg

subject: FWD: West Coast Power Project - Go Decision
body:
-------- Forwarded Message --------
From: jeff.skilling@enron.com
To: ken.lay@enron.com, andy.fastow@enron.com
Subject: West Coast Power Project - Go Decision

Ken, Andy,

After reviewing the risk models with Greg Whalley, I've decided we should move forward with the West Coast Power project. Target capacity is 800 MW. We'll need Andy to close the Raptors financing by end of Q1.

Assigning project lead to Louise Kitchen. She has full authority to negotiate contracts.

Let's not discuss this over email going forward.

- Jeff

subject: FERC ruling expected this week
body:
Louise,

Heard from our DC contact that FERC will rule on the Oregon transmission limits by Dec 13. If they approve expanded capacity, we can hold at 800 MW with no issues. If not, we trigger the Nevada fallback Greg approved.

Stay tuned.

Tim
